commit.message:
feat: pre-create all Mode 01 and Mode 09 PID sensors at install time

Previously sensors appeared dynamically as SSE readings arrived, so a
freshly-installed integration showed no PID sensors until the car was on
and data was flowing.  Now all 128 Mode 01 and 12 Mode 09 sensors are
created immediately at setup with a None initial value and populate live
as readings arrive (or restore from HA storage on restart).

Changes:
- const.py: Add MODE01_PIDS and MODE09_PIDS as list[tuple[str, str|None]]
  sourced from the Rust PID registry in src-tauri/src/db/mode01.rs and
  mode09.rs. Also add missing units (s, min, Nm, ppm, λ, °BTDC, °) to
  UNIT_DEVICE_CLASS_MAP and UNIT_STATE_CLASS_MAP.
- sensor.py: CanteraSensor constructor now takes (name, unit) instead of
  initial_reading dict. async_setup_entry creates all 140 PID sensors plus
  the sync-status sensor upfront; the dynamic _on_reading factory listener
  is removed.
- tests/test_sensor.py: Updated for new constructor, added tests for
  pre-creation count, None initial values, Mode 01/09 coverage, and
  no-listener-in-setup assertion.

Total sensors after install: 141 (128 Mode01 + 12 Mode09 + 1 sync status)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: auto-memory system — post-commit hook + memory logger + pre-push check

3-layer automated memory documentation system:

1. POST-COMMIT HOOK (backend/scripts/post-commit-logger.js)
   - Runs automatically after every git commit
   - Appends structured entry to memories/repo/auto-commit-log.md
   - Records: timestamp, hash, message, files, categories, memory-update status
   - Categories auto-detected: API, SERVICE, DB, UI, i18n, BUILD, DEPLOY, etc.

2. MEMORY LOGGER SCRIPT (backend/scripts/log-memory.js)
   - One-command structured logging for bugs, lessons, features, research, notes
   - node backend/scripts/log-memory.js bug|lesson|feature|research|note ...
   - npm shortcuts: npm run log:bug, log:lesson, log:feature, log:research

3. PRE-PUSH MEMORY CHECK (pre-deploy.js check #5)
   - Warns if code files changed but no memories/ file was updated
   - Non-blocking (warning only) so it never stops a legitimate push

4. COPILOT INSTRUCTIONS UPDATED (.github/copilot-instructions.md)
   - Documents all memory files and their purpose
   - Adds auto-commit-log.md to the agent's start-of-session reads

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: support go install and fix devcontainer (#39)

* feat: support go install by moving CLI entrypoint to repo root

Move package main from cmd/ to the repository root so that
`go install github.com/manisbindra/sbi@latest` produces a binary
named `sbi` instead of the unusable `cmd`.

- Move all cmd/*.go files to repo root, remove cmd/ directory
- Rename CLI identity from daily-recommendations to sbi
- Update Taskfile.yml: project name and build path
- Update .goreleaser.yml: project name, IDs, remove dir: cmd
- Update CI workflows: binary paths in nightly-scan and scan-smoke-test
- Update README.md: add go install section, update CLI examples
- Update AGENTS.md: binary name, examples, project structure

Closes #38

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add sudo to devcontainer install-tools.sh for /usr/local/bin writes

onCreateCommand runs as the vscode user which lacks write permissions
to /usr/local/bin. Add sudo to the syft, trivy, and golangci-lint
install commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update devcontainer Go image from 1.24 to 1.26

go.mod requires go >= 1.26.1 but the devcontainer image was pinned to
Go 1.24, causing build and test failures inside the container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update go.mod to Go 1.26.2 to resolve stdlib vulnerabilities

Go 1.26.1 has known vulnerabilities in crypto/x509, crypto/tls,
archive/tar, and html/template (GO-2026-4864 through GO-2026-4947),
all fixed in Go 1.26.2. govulncheck now passes cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- Update reset-database.yml binary path from daily-recommendations to sbi
- Fix goreleaser archive: repositories.txt → repositories.json
- README: restructure Quick Start to use go install, add Build from Source
- AGENTS.md: add go install command to CLI Usage section

commit.message:
[experimental] Module images (#3944)

* [experimental] Reorganize module-images and add pre-commit validation

Move module images into per-module subdirectories:
  module-images/<manufacturer>/<module-type>/<image>

Add test_module_images check enforcing structure, naming, YAML
existence, and 2-image limit for newly added module images.

Refs: #3776

* module-images: flatten per-model subdirs to vendor-level, keep front/rear only

Per PR #3944 review:
- Move all images from per-model subdirs to flat vendor directories
- Rename kept images to follow <model>.front.<ext> / <model>.rear.<ext> convention
- Delete images that do not show a clean orthogonal front or rear face view
  (angled shots, marketing images, side/top/internal views)
- Vendors affected: Check Point, Cisco, CyberPower, Dell, Eltex, MegaTec, MikroTik, Panduit, ZTE

* tests: update test_module_images for flat vendor-dir structure

- Expect module-images/<manufacturer>/<filename> (3 parts, not 4)
- Extract module_name from filename via (front|rear) pattern match
- Enforce <module-name>.(front|rear).<ext> naming convention
- Image count check now scans vendor dir for files matching module prefix
- Expand CHANGE_TYPE_LIST to ['A', 'R', 'M', 'T'] — pre-existing images
  with wrong naming have been cleaned up, renames are now safe to validate

* docs: update Module Type Images section for flat vendor-dir structure

- Path is now module-images/<manufacturer>/<filename> (no per-model subdir)
- Naming requires (front|rear) qualifier: <module-type>.(front|rear).<ext>
- Added content rule: orthogonal front/rear face views only

* Update README.md

- On first route: auto-select hintEp if found in CMS episodes, else EP1
- Route switching: keeps currentEp, falls back to EP1 if not in new route
- Episode list rendered from currentRoute.episodes (CMS data)

commit.message:
Remove fastText support and enable numpy 2.x compatibility (#1315)

* Remove fastText support and enable numpy 2.x compatibility

FastText library has been archived since March 2024 and is no longer
maintained. This dependency was blocking numpy 2.x adoption.

Changes:
- Remove fasttext extra from setup.py dependencies
- Add deprecation warnings to fasttext.py module
- Update documentation to recommend langdetect as primary option
- Update CLI to deprecate --fasttext flag
- Add comprehensive test suite for langdetect integration
- Remove fasttext from tox.ini test configurations

Users should migrate to langdetect:
  pip install dateparser[langdetect]

Closes #1287
Closes #1251

* Update dateparser_cli/cli.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update dateparser/custom_language_detection/fasttext.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify fasttext deprecation to always error

The previous approach was confusing:
- If fasttext was installed, it showed a warning but still worked
- If fasttext wasn't installed, it raised an ImportError

This creates inconsistent deprecation - users don't know if the
feature is truly deprecated or not.

Now the module:
- Always shows deprecation warning on import
- Always raises ImportError when detect_languages() is called
- Provides clear migration path to langdetect
- No functional fasttext code remains

This makes the deprecation clear and consistent.

* Remove unused module-level mock in test_language_detect

The module-level 'detect_languages' mock was defined but never used.
The MockLangDetectTest class defines its own mock as a class attribute,
and other test classes use the actual lang_detect_detect_languages function.

This cleanup removes the unused mock to avoid confusion.

commit.message:
fix: split Vite bundle to prevent Render free-tier OOM (status 2)

Root cause: Render free tier has 512MB RAM. Vite minifying a single 1,585KB
bundle exceeded available memory, causing OOM kill reported as 'status 2
internal system error'.

Fix: manual chunk splitting in vite.config.ts splits vendor libs into separate
files. Rollup minifies each chunk independently so peak RAM = largest_chunk
not total_bundle.

Result: index.js 1,585KB → 1,174KB. New vendor chunks:
  vendor-react:    163KB (react, react-dom, react-router-dom)
  vendor-maps:     155KB (leaflet, react-leaflet)
  vendor-markdown: 117KB (react-markdown)
  vendor-i18n:      61KB (i18next + plugins)
  vendor-socket:    42KB (socket.io-client)
  vendor-query:     29KB (@tanstack/react-query)

Also reverted render-build.sh back to npm install (more resilient than
npm ci on flaky network connections in free-tier Render builds).

commit.message:
fix: close autonomous review loop for agentic PRs

Root cause: agentic workflow PRs were auto-expiring after 2-3 days
because the review loop was broken:
- pr-docs-reviewer skipped agentic PRs (skip-bots: [github-actions])
- pr-docs-reviewer could only comment, not approve/reject
- No approval → auto-merge never triggered → PRs expired

Changes:
- Upgrade pr-docs-reviewer to autonomous reviewer:
  - Remove github-actions from skip-bots
  - Add submit-pull-request-review (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES)
  - Add create-pull-request-review-comment for inline feedback
  - Update prompt: approve on pass, request changes on 🔴 issues
- Enable auto-merge + draft:false on 4 workflows missing it:
  docs-vnext-sync, glossary-maintainer, slide-deck-maintainer,
  label-ops-docs-fix
- Extend PR expiry from 2d/3d to 7d across all 9 PR-creating
  workflows as safety net
- Remove discussion-responder workflow (GitHub Discussions not
  enabled on this repo)

Expected flow: workflow creates PR → pr-docs-reviewer reviews →
APPROVE → auto-merge completes

commit.message:
feat: counter-trend penalty — penalize signals opposing BTC trend

COUNTER_TREND_PENALTY multiplier applied after BTC_TREND_WEIGHTS.
During TREND_UP: SHORT signals × 0.5 (effectively blocked).
During TREND_DOWN: LONG signals × 0.5 (effectively blocked).
Mean-reversion passports (BBMeanRev, RSIContrarian, MACDDivergence,
ReversalV2, Reversal) override to 1.0 — they trade freely counter-trend.

This fixes the architectural gap where 77-92% of live trades were
counter-trend SHORTs during BTC uptrend, causing -59% portfolio loss.

Changes:
- bot/config.py: COUNTER_TREND_PENALTY config dict
- bot/scorer.py: apply penalty in score_confluence()
- bot/research/extended_scorer.py: same penalty logic
- bot/passport_runner.py: add CTP to save/restore keys
- 5 passport JSONs: mean-reversion CTP=1.0 override
- 7 new tests (test_counter_trend_penalty.py)
- Fix test_calc_audit.py to isolate CTP from BTW tests

commit.message:
perf: pool value vectors, flatten child_vals, persist scratch across iterations

Reduce per-node allocation churn in the range-vector CFR traversal:

- Extend ReachVectorScratch with values and child_flat buffer pools
- Replace per-node vec![] allocations in traverse_rv with pooled take/give
- Replace Vec<Vec<f32>> child_vals with flat contiguous buffer (n_actions * n_target)
- Refactor terminal_values_rv to write into pre-allocated buffer
- Persist ReachVectorScratch across iterations in the main solve loop
- Add PendingAccumulator::zero() for future cross-iteration reuse

Measured ~5.5% solve throughput improvement on 1x1 tiny fixture (11.9ms vs 12.6ms).
Larger gains expected on realistic spots with 50+ combos per side.

fix(nu): gracefully skip init when oh-my-posh is not installed (#7442)

* fix(nu): add early return guard when oh-my-posh is not available

If a user uninstalls oh-my-posh without removing the autoload script,
the script would still run on every Nu startup and produce errors.

Add an early return guard at the top of omp.nu that exits the script
immediately if the executable is no longer available. Handles both
install modes:
- Absolute path (standard install): \path exists\
- Base name only (MSIX / --strict mode): \which\

Fixes #7245

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(msi): accept WiX v7 EULA in build script

WiX v7 now requires explicit EULA acceptance under the Open Source
Maintenance Fee policy. Add -acceptEula wix7 to the wix build command
so CI releases don't fail.

See: https://docs.firegiant.com/wix/osmf/#direct-acceptance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(nu): guard path exists check to absolute/relative paths only

When the executable is stored as a bare name (e.g. MSIX / --strict mode),
\path exists\ resolves against the current working directory and may
incorrectly return true if an unrelated file with the same name exists
there. Only apply the \path exists\ check when the executable string
looks like a path (contains a separator or starts with . / ~); otherwise
rely solely on \which\ for the PATH-based lookup.

commit.message:
fix(hostexec): resolve relative argv0 against cwd for rule matching

When an agent runs `cd backend && ./gradlew spotlessApply`, the Python
wrapper sends argv0="./gradlew" with cwd="/workspace/backend". The rule
defines argv0="./backend/gradlew" (workspace-root-relative). matchRule
compared these directly and failed to match, causing a fallback error:
"relative argv0 fallback is not supported".

Fix matchRule to accept an optional MatchContext (cwd + workspaceRoot).
When direct relative-path comparison fails, resolve the actual argv0
against cwd to an absolute path, then re-derive the workspace-relative
path for comparison. This is done in the broker (TS) where both cwd and
workspaceRoot are already available, avoiding changes to the Python
wrapper or new environment variables.

commit.message:
anime: fix mp4 direct-link playback + proper player height

- Server: add 'type' field (hls/mp4) to each source based on raw URL
- Frontend: detect mp4/flv URLs and use video.src directly instead of HLS.js
  (HLS.js manifestLoadError was caused by feeding mp4 URLs to HLS.js)
- CSS: video container fixed to min(45vh, 460px), video scales inside via
  height:100%/width:auto so player is compact and proportional on any screen
- Restored .animePlayerPageHeader selector that was accidentally dropped
- .animePlayerPageScroll handles overflow for source bar + episodes

commit.message:
Add RLPRFilter and posterior_predictive_moments refactor

- Add make_runlength_state factory to states.py (cached, analogous to
  make_greedy_runlength_state) for dynamic runlength state creation
- Refactor ExtendedFilter.predictive_density to extract
  posterior_predictive_moments, returning mean and covariance separately
- Add RLPRFilter: a generic Runlength prior-reset filter that fuses any
  filter belief state with runlength tracking via make_runlength_state
